page: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It also includes 1890 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the E32ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and single-threaded compression/decompression tests placing in the top 10%. Multi-threaded performance is strong, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score in the top 25% and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and memory bandwidth (both cached and uncached) align with average middle-tier results. For database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, the instance delivers average throughput. While the server handles small-scale LLM inference efficiently, achieving top-tier text generation speeds for a 135M parameter model, it exhibits a significant performance tradeoff on larger models, dropping to a poor rating of 2.46 tokens per second for 70B model text generation due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E32ads_v7 offers a high-density memory profile paired with dedicated compute resources and substantial local NVMe storage, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads without the premium cost of GPU-accelerated hardware. The combination of high single-core performance, large memory capacity, and local storage makes this instance highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive development or compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, alongside 1890 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, its multi-core processing and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier, and it exhibits poor performance for large-scale LLM inference due to the absence of a GPU. Qualitatively, the E32ads_v7 offers a cost-effective, high-density memory profile with dedicated compute resources. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage for database and analytical workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, substantial local SSD storage, and top-tier single-core performance.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz.
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio.
1890 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
Lacks GPU acceleration, resulting in poor performance for large LLM inference.
